mission.title: backstage: [Plugin] Datadog Integration
mission.description: A Datadog plugin for Backstage that embeds Datadog dashboards, metrics, and graphs directly into the Backstage service catalog, allowing developers to monitor service health from a single pane of glass. Requested by 42+ users.

steps.title: Check current Backstage setup
steps.description:
Verify your Backstage installation is running:
```bash
# If running via Docker:
docker ps | grep backstage

# If deployed on Kubernetes:
kubectl get pods -n backstage
```
This plugin requires a running Backstage instance and a Datadog account.

steps.title: Install the Datadog plugin
steps.description:
Add the Datadog plugin to your Backstage app. A community-maintained plugin is available from Roadie:
```bash
cd packages/app
yarn add @roadiehq/backstage-plugin-datadog
```
See the Backstage Plugin Marketplace for the latest available Datadog integration plugins.

steps.title: Configure the Datadog integration
steps.description:
Annotate your catalog entities with the Datadog dashboard URL:
```yaml
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  annotations:
    datadoghq.com/dashboard-url: 'https://p.datadoghq.com/sb/your-dashboard-id'
```
The plugin embeds Datadog dashboards as iframes in the entity page. You may need to configure Datadog's embeddable/public dashboard sharing for this to work.

steps.title: Verify the Datadog plugin works
steps.description:
Open Backstage and navigate to a service entity that has Datadog annotations:
1. Check that the Datadog tab or card appears on the entity page
2. Verify that the embedded Datadog dashboard loads correctly
3. Confirm that metrics and graphs from Datadog are visible

resolution.summary: A community-maintained Datadog plugin for Backstage embeds Datadog dashboards and graphs directly into the service catalog entity pages. Annotate catalog entities with datadoghq.com/dashboard-url to link them to embeddable Datadog dashboards.

prerequisites.description: A running Backstage instance (via Docker, npx @backstage/create-app, or Kubernetes) and a Datadog account with dashboard sharing enabled.
